title: Colour reference
text: Click swatch gradient strip copy hex code clipboard.

title: Diverging palettes
text: Nine stops, multi-hue. High perceptual contrast across full range.
code: # Correlation matrix vars    <- c("mpg", "cyl", "disp", "hp", "drat", "wt", "qsec") cor_mat <- cor(mtcars[, vars]) cor_df  <- data.frame(   Var1  = rep(vars, each  = length(vars)),   Var2  = rep(vars, times = length(vars)),   value = as.vector(cor_mat) )  ggplot(cor_df, aes(Var1, Var2, fill = value)) +   geom_tile(colour = "white", linewidth = 0.5) +   geom_text(aes(label = round(value, 2)), size = 3, colour = "white") +   scale_fill_circadia_c("diverging", limits = c(-1, 1)) +   labs(title = "Motor Trend — variable correlations",        fill = "r", x = NULL, y = NULL) +   theme_circadia(grid = "none") +   theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

title: Diverging palettes
text: Seven stops, direct blue → antique white → coral interpolation. Lower chromatic complexity — cleaner small multiples print.
code: ggplot(cor_df, aes(Var1, Var2, fill = value)) +   geom_tile(colour = "white", linewidth = 0.5) +   geom_text(aes(label = round(value, 2)), size = 3, colour = "#333333") +   scale_fill_circadia_c("diverging_simple", limits = c(-1, 1)) +   labs(title = "Motor Trend — variable correlations (simple palette)",        fill = "r", x = NULL, y = NULL) +   theme_circadia(grid = "none") +   theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

title: Diverging palettes
code: library(patchwork)  # Standardised sleep-like data: z-scores across a small grid set.seed(42) grid_df <- expand.grid(x = 1:12, y = 1:8) grid_df$z <- as.vector(scale(rnorm(96, mean = grid_df$x - 6, sd = 2)))  make_tile <- function(pal) {   ggplot(grid_df, aes(x, y, fill = z)) +     geom_tile() +     scale_fill_circadia_c(pal, limits = c(-3, 3)) +     labs(title = paste0('palette = "', pal, '"'), x = NULL, y = NULL, fill = "z") +     theme_circadia(grid = "none") +     theme(axis.text = element_blank(), axis.ticks = element_blank()) }  make_tile("diverging") | make_tile("diverging_simple")

title: Diverging palettes
text: Use limits argument (passed scale_fill_gradientn()) pin palette midpoint specific value:
code: # Centre the palette at 0 with symmetric limits scale_fill_circadia_c("diverging", limits = c(-1, 1))  # Asymmetric data — still centre at 0 scale_fill_circadia_c("diverging", limits = c(-3, 3))

title: Getting started with circadia
text: Retrieve palette name circadia_palette(): palettes support reverse = TRUE sub-setting via n:
code: circadia_palettes()   # list all available palettes #> Available circadia palettes: #>   Qualitative: #>     main                  8 colours #>     core                  5 colours #>   Diverging: #>     diverging             9 colours #>     diverging_simple      7 colours #>   Sequential (complex): #>     blues                 6 colours #>     warm                  5 colours #>   Sequential (simple): #>     seq_blue              5 colours #>     seq_coral             5 colours #>     seq_amber             5 colours #>     seq_ochre             5 colours  circadia_palette()          # main (6 colours, default) #>     deep_blue     coral_red         amber         ochre antique_white  #>     "#014370"     "#FC544A"     "#FFA75D"     "#C8860A"     "#FFECD4"  #>      mid_blue    steel_blue     pale_teal  #>     "#1B6799"     "#4A9BBF"     "#9BDFE2" circadia_palette("core")    # compact 4-colour subset #>     deep_blue     coral_red         amber         ochre antique_white  #>     "#014370"     "#FC544A"     "#FFA75D"     "#C8860A"     "#FFECD4" circadia_palette("blues", n = 4) #> [1] "#014370" "#1B6799" "#4A9BBF" "#7FB5C8" circadia_palette("diverging", reverse = TRUE) #> [1] "#FC544A" "#FC7060" "#FFA75D" "#FFC99A" "#FFECD4" "#9BDFE2" "#4A9BBF" #> [8] "#1B6799" "#014370"

title: Getting started with circadia
text: domain_colour_for() maps data domains brand colour — useful annotating panels data type:
code: domain_colour_for("actigraphy") #> actigraphy  #>  "#014370" domain_colour_for("sleep") #>     sleep  #> "#1B6799" domain_colour_for("questionnaire") #> questionnaire  #>     "#FFA75D"

title: Getting started with circadia
text: Apply theme_circadia() ggplot2 plot:  grid argument controls gridlines shown:
code: ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg, colour = factor(cyl))) +   geom_point(size = 3) +   labs(     title    = "Motor Trend Car Road Tests",     subtitle = "Weight vs fuel efficiency by cylinder count",     colour   = "Cylinders",     x = "Weight (1000 lbs)", y = "Miles per gallon"   ) +   scale_colour_circadia() +   theme_circadia() ggplot(mpg, aes(class, fill = drv)) +   geom_bar() +   scale_fill_circadia() +   theme_circadia(grid = "y", legend_position = "top")

title: Getting started with circadia
text: continuous data use scale_fill_circadia_c() scale_colour_circadia_c(). "diverging" palette suits centred data; "blues" "warm" suit unipolar data.
code: ggplot(faithfuld, aes(waiting, eruptions, fill = density)) +   geom_tile() +   scale_fill_circadia_c("warm") +   theme_circadia(grid = "none")

title: Qualitative palettes
text: Eight brand colours spanning full chromatic range — use 8 categorical groups. Click swatch copy hex code.
code: ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg, colour = factor(cyl))) +   geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.85) +   labs(     title   = "Fuel efficiency by engine size",     colour  = "Cylinders",     x       = "Weight (1000 lbs)",     y       = "Miles per gallon"   ) +   scale_colour_circadia() +   theme_circadia() ggplot(mpg, aes(class, fill = drv)) +   geom_bar(position = "dodge") +   labs(     title = "Vehicle classes by drive type",     fill  = "Drive",     x     = NULL, y = "Count"   ) +   scale_fill_circadia() +   theme_circadia(grid = "y")

title: Qualitative palettes
text: compact 5-colour subset — four anchors plus ochre. Use figures five fewer groups want cleanest possible colour separation.
code: ggplot(mpg, aes(displ, hwy, colour = drv)) +   geom_point(size = 2.5, alpha = 0.8) +   labs(     title  = "Engine displacement vs highway mpg",     colour = "Drive type",     x      = "Displacement (L)", y = "Highway mpg"   ) +   scale_colour_circadia(palette = "core") +   theme_circadia()

title: Qualitative palettes
text: Pass reverse = TRUE flip colour order — useful want lightest colour map first factor level.
code: ggplot(mtcars, aes(factor(cyl), mpg, fill = factor(cyl))) +   geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.85, show.legend = FALSE) +   labs(     title = "MPG distribution by cylinders — reversed palette",     x     = "Cylinders", y = "Miles per gallon"   ) +   scale_fill_circadia(palette = "core", reverse = TRUE) +   theme_circadia(grid = "y")

title: Qualitative palettes
text: Use circadia_palette() need raw hex values — example, pass ggplot2::scale_colour_manual() base R graphics.
code: # Full main palette circadia_palette("main") #>     deep_blue     coral_red         amber         ochre antique_white  #>     "#014370"     "#FC544A"     "#FFA75D"     "#C8860A"     "#FFECD4"  #>      mid_blue    steel_blue     pale_teal  #>     "#1B6799"     "#4A9BBF"     "#9BDFE2"  # First three colours from core circadia_palette("core", n = 3) #> deep_blue coral_red     amber  #> "#014370" "#FC544A" "#FFA75D"  # Reversed blues circadia_palette("core", reverse = TRUE) #> antique_white         ochre         amber     coral_red     deep_blue  #>     "#FFECD4"     "#C8860A"     "#FFA75D"     "#FC544A"     "#014370"

title: Sequential palettes
text: Six stops deep blue pale teal — suited density, intensity, frequency data.
code: ggplot(faithfuld, aes(waiting, eruptions, fill = density)) +   geom_tile() +   scale_fill_circadia_c("blues") +   labs(title = "Old Faithful eruption density — blues",        x = "Waiting time (min)", y = "Eruption duration (min)") +   theme_circadia(grid = "none")

title: Sequential palettes
text: Five stops coral antique white — suited warm-toned data body temperature, light exposure, alertness ratings.
code: ggplot(faithfuld, aes(waiting, eruptions, fill = density)) +   geom_tile() +   scale_fill_circadia_c("warm") +   labs(title = "Old Faithful eruption density — warm",        x = "Waiting time (min)", y = "Eruption duration (min)") +   theme_circadia(grid = "none")

title: Sequential palettes
text: simple palette monochromatic ramp one brand colour, running pale tint full saturated value. Use hue carries meaning — e.g. seq_blue sleep depth, seq_ochre light exposure intensity. seq_blue seq_coral seq_amber seq_ochre
code: make_tile <- function(pal) {   ggplot(faithfuld, aes(waiting, eruptions, fill = density)) +     geom_tile() +     scale_fill_circadia_c(pal) +     labs(title = pal, x = NULL, y = NULL) +     theme_circadia(grid = "none") +     theme(       plot.title = element_text(size = 11),       axis.text  = element_blank(),       axis.ticks = element_blank(),       legend.position = "none"     ) }  (make_tile("seq_blue") + make_tile("seq_coral")) / (make_tile("seq_amber") + make_tile("seq_ochre"))

title: Sequential palettes
text: continuous scales compose naturally theme_circadia():
code: ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg, colour = hp)) +   geom_point(size = 3) +   scale_colour_circadia_c("blues") +   labs(     title  = "Weight, efficiency and horsepower",     colour = "HP",     x      = "Weight (1000 lbs)", y = "Miles per gallon"   ) +   theme_circadia()

title: Sequential palettes
text: Pass reverse = TRUE continuous scale flip direction — useful lower values map saturated end:
code: ggplot(faithfuld, aes(waiting, eruptions, fill = density)) +   geom_tile() +   scale_fill_circadia_c("blues", reverse = TRUE) +   labs(title = "Reversed blues — high density = light",        x = "Waiting time (min)", y = "Eruption duration (min)") +   theme_circadia(grid = "none")

what: circadia — Circadia Lab colour system for R
title: circadia — Circadia Lab colour system for R
text: Shared colour palettes, ggplot2 theme, scales Circadia Lab R ecosystem.

title: circadia — Circadia Lab colour system for R
text: circadia visual identity layer Circadia Lab R ecosystem. provides consistent set brand colours, clean ggplot2 theme, discrete continuous scale functions plug directly plots produced zeitR, slumbR, tallieR, syncR, standalone analysis notebooks.

title: circadia — Circadia Lab colour system for R
text: 🎨 Five named palettes — main (6-colour qualitative), core (4-colour compact), diverging (blue → antique white → coral), blues (sequential), warm (sequential) 🔬 Domain colour map — domain_colour_for() returns brand colour named data domain ("actigraphy", "sleep", "circadian", "questionnaire", "demographics", "clinical") 📊 Discrete scales — scale_colour_circadia() scale_fill_circadia() categorical data 🌈 Continuous scales — scale_colour_circadia_c() scale_fill_circadia_c() colour interpolation 🖼 theme_circadia() — minimal ggplot2 theme configurable gridlines, Circadia Lab typography, brand accent colours

title: circadia — Circadia Lab colour system for R
code: circadia/ ├── R/ │   ├── circadia-package.R   # package-level documentation │   ├── palettes.R           # palette definitions and retrieval functions │   ├── scales.R             # ggplot2 discrete and continuous scales │   └── theme.R              # theme_circadia() ├── tests/ │   └── testthat/ │       ├── test-palettes.R │       └── test-scales.R ├── vignettes/ │   └── getting-started.Rmd ├── _pkgdown.yml ├── DESCRIPTION └── LICENSE

title: circadia — Circadia Lab colour system for R
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("circadia-bio/circadia")

title: circadia — Circadia Lab colour system for R
code: library(circadia) library(ggplot2)  # Retrieve a palette circadia_palette("core") #> deep_blue    coral_red        amber antique_white #> "#014370"   "#FC544A"   "#FFA75D"   "#FFECD4"  # Domain colour lookup domain_colour_for("sleep") #>     sleep #> "#1B6799"  # Apply theme and scales to a plot ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg, colour = factor(cyl))) +   geom_point(size = 3) +   scale_colour_circadia() +   theme_circadia()  # Continuous fill scale with the diverging palette ggplot(faithfuld, aes(waiting, eruptions, fill = density)) +   geom_tile() +   scale_fill_circadia_c("warm") +   theme_circadia(grid = "none")

title: circadia — Circadia Lab colour system for R
text: ⏱ zeitR — wrist actigraphy analysis circadian metrics 😴 slumbR — sleep diary processing 📋 tallieR — sociodemographic questionnaire data 🔄 syncR — unified participant-indexed database 🔬 circadia-bio — Circadia Lab GitHub organisation

what: circadia: Circadia Lab Colour Palettes and ggplot2 Theme — circadia-package
title: circadia: Circadia Lab Colour Palettes and ggplot2 Theme — circadia-package
text: Provides shared visual identity Circadia Lab R ecosystem: colour palettes, ggplot2 theme, ggplot2 scales. Palettes cover qualitative, sequential, diverging use cases anchored lab's brand colours. Designed used alongside zeitR, slumbR, tallieR, syncR.

title: circadia: Circadia Lab Colour Palettes and ggplot2 Theme — circadia-package
text: Maintainer: Lucas França lucas.franca@northumbria.ac.uk (ORCID) Authors: Mario Leocadio-Miguel mario.miguel@northumbria.ac.uk (ORCID)

what: Retrieve a Circadia Lab palette — circadia_palette
title: Retrieve a Circadia Lab palette — circadia_palette
text: Returns named character vector hex colour codes requested palette. Suitable direct use ggplot2::scale_fill_manual() ggplot2::scale_colour_manual().

title: Retrieve a Circadia Lab palette — circadia_palette
code: circadia_palette(palette = "main", n = NULL, reverse = FALSE)

title: Retrieve a Circadia Lab palette — circadia_palette
text: palette Name palette. One "main", "core", "diverging", "diverging_simple", "blues", "warm", "seq_blue", "seq_coral", "seq_amber", "seq_ochre". Defaults "main". n Number colours return. NULL (default) colours palette returned. n smaller palette length first n colours returned; larger error thrown. reverse Logical. Reverse order palette? Default FALSE.

title: Retrieve a Circadia Lab palette — circadia_palette
text: named (unnamed gradient palettes) character vector hex colour codes.

title: Retrieve a Circadia Lab palette — circadia_palette
text: Gradient palettes come two families: Complex (multi-hue): "diverging", "blues", "warm" Simple (monochromatic / direct interpolation): "diverging_simple", "seq_blue", "seq_coral", "seq_amber", "seq_ochre"

title: Retrieve a Circadia Lab palette — circadia_palette
code: circadia_palette() #>     deep_blue     coral_red         amber         ochre antique_white  #>     "#014370"     "#FC544A"     "#FFA75D"     "#C8860A"     "#FFECD4"  #>      mid_blue    steel_blue     pale_teal  #>     "#1B6799"     "#4A9BBF"     "#9BDFE2"  circadia_palette("core") #>     deep_blue     coral_red         amber         ochre antique_white  #>     "#014370"     "#FC544A"     "#FFA75D"     "#C8860A"     "#FFECD4"  circadia_palette("diverging_simple") #> [1] "#014370" "#567B91" "#AAB4B3" "#FFECD4" "#FEB9A6" "#FD8778" "#FC544A" circadia_palette("seq_blue", n = 3) #> [1] "#D4E5EF" "#8FBDD5" "#4A95BB" circadia_palette("diverging", reverse = TRUE) #> [1] "#FC544A" "#FC7060" "#FFA75D" "#FFC99A" "#FFECD4" "#9BDFE2" "#4A9BBF" #> [8] "#1B6799" "#014370"

what: List all available Circadia Lab palettes — circadia_palettes
title: List all available Circadia Lab palettes — circadia_palettes
text: Prints names sizes palettes defined package.

title: List all available Circadia Lab palettes — circadia_palettes
code: circadia_palettes()

title: List all available Circadia Lab palettes — circadia_palettes
text: named integer vector palette lengths, invisibly.

title: List all available Circadia Lab palettes — circadia_palettes
code: circadia_palettes() #> Available circadia palettes: #>   Qualitative: #>     main                  8 colours #>     core                  5 colours #>   Diverging: #>     diverging             9 colours #>     diverging_simple      7 colours #>   Sequential (complex): #>     blues                 6 colours #>     warm                  5 colours #>   Sequential (simple): #>     seq_blue              5 colours #>     seq_coral             5 colours #>     seq_amber             5 colours #>     seq_ochre             5 colours

what: Return the brand colour for a Circadia Lab data domain — domain_colour_for
title: Return the brand colour for a Circadia Lab data domain — domain_colour_for
text: Looks hex colour associated named data domain. Useful want colour-code panels annotations data type rather group membership.

title: Return the brand colour for a Circadia Lab data domain — domain_colour_for
code: domain_colour_for(domain)

title: Return the brand colour for a Circadia Lab data domain — domain_colour_for
text: domain Character scalar. One "actigraphy", "sleep", "circadian", "questionnaire", "demographics", "clinical", "light", "activity".

title: Return the brand colour for a Circadia Lab data domain — domain_colour_for
text: length-1 named character vector domain hex colour, e.g. c(actigraphy = "#014370").

title: Return the brand colour for a Circadia Lab data domain — domain_colour_for
code: domain_colour_for("sleep") #>     sleep  #> "#1B6799"  domain_colour_for("light") #>     light  #> "#C8860A"  domain_colour_for("activity") #>  activity  #> "#4A9BBF"

title: Domain colour map — .circadia_domain_info
text: Associates data domain Circadia Lab ecosystem brand colour. Consumed internally domain_colour_for().

title: Domain colour map — .circadia_domain_info
code: .circadia_domain_info

title: Domain colour map — .circadia_domain_info
text: object class list length 8.

what: Build a palette function from a Circadia palette — .circadia_pal
title: Build a palette function from a Circadia palette — .circadia_pal

code: .circadia_pal(palette = "main", reverse = FALSE)

title: Build a palette function from a Circadia palette — .circadia_pal
text: palette Palette name passed circadia_palette(). reverse Logical. Reverse palette?

title: Circadia Lab colour palettes — .circadia_palettes
text: Named list palettes available circadia package.

title: Circadia Lab colour palettes — .circadia_palettes
code: .circadia_palettes

title: Circadia Lab colour palettes — .circadia_palettes
text: named list character vectors, containing hex colour codes.

what: Circadia Lab discrete colour scale — scale_colour_circadia
title: Circadia Lab discrete colour scale — scale_colour_circadia
text: Applies Circadia Lab qualitative palette colour aesthetic.

title: Circadia Lab discrete colour scale — scale_colour_circadia
code: scale_colour_circadia(palette = "main", reverse = FALSE, ...)  scale_color_circadia(palette = "main", reverse = FALSE, ...)

title: Circadia Lab discrete colour scale — scale_colour_circadia
text: palette Palette name. Default "main". reverse Logical. Reverse palette? Default FALSE. ... Additional arguments passed ggplot2::discrete_scale().

title: Circadia Lab discrete colour scale — scale_colour_circadia
code: library(ggplot2) ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg, colour = factor(cyl))) +   geom_point(size = 3) +   scale_colour_circadia() +   theme_circadia()

what: Circadia Lab continuous colour scale — scale_colour_circadia_c
title: Circadia Lab continuous colour scale — scale_colour_circadia_c
text: Interpolates across Circadia Lab palette continuous colour data. "diverging" palette recommended data centred zero; "blues" "warm" unipolar data.

title: Circadia Lab continuous colour scale — scale_colour_circadia_c
code: scale_colour_circadia_c(palette = "blues", reverse = FALSE, ...)  scale_color_circadia_c(palette = "blues", reverse = FALSE, ...)

title: Circadia Lab continuous colour scale — scale_colour_circadia_c
text: palette Palette name. Default "blues". reverse Logical. Reverse palette? Default FALSE. ... Additional arguments passed ggplot2::scale_colour_gradientn().

title: Circadia Lab continuous colour scale — scale_colour_circadia_c
code: library(ggplot2) ggplot(faithfuld, aes(waiting, eruptions, fill = density)) +   geom_tile() +   scale_fill_circadia_c("warm") +   theme_circadia()

what: Circadia Lab discrete fill scale — scale_fill_circadia
title: Circadia Lab discrete fill scale — scale_fill_circadia
text: Applies Circadia Lab qualitative palette fill aesthetic.

title: Circadia Lab discrete fill scale — scale_fill_circadia
code: scale_fill_circadia(palette = "main", reverse = FALSE, ...)

title: Circadia Lab discrete fill scale — scale_fill_circadia
text: palette Palette name. Default "main". reverse Logical. Reverse palette? Default FALSE. ... Additional arguments passed ggplot2::discrete_scale().

title: Circadia Lab discrete fill scale — scale_fill_circadia
code: library(ggplot2) ggplot(mpg, aes(class, fill = drv)) +   geom_bar() +   scale_fill_circadia() +   theme_circadia()

what: Circadia Lab continuous fill scale — scale_fill_circadia_c
title: Circadia Lab continuous fill scale — scale_fill_circadia_c
text: Interpolates across Circadia Lab palette continuous fill data.

title: Circadia Lab continuous fill scale — scale_fill_circadia_c
code: scale_fill_circadia_c(palette = "blues", reverse = FALSE, ...)

title: Circadia Lab continuous fill scale — scale_fill_circadia_c
text: palette Palette name. Default "blues". reverse Logical. Reverse palette? Default FALSE. ... Additional arguments passed ggplot2::scale_colour_gradientn().

title: Circadia Lab ggplot2 theme — theme_circadia
text: clean, minimal ggplot2 theme built ggplot2::theme_minimal() typography spacing consistent Circadia Lab figures.

title: Circadia Lab ggplot2 theme — theme_circadia
code: theme_circadia(   base_size = 12,   base_family = "",   grid = "xy",   legend_position = "right" )

title: Circadia Lab ggplot2 theme — theme_circadia
text: base_size Base font size points. Default 12. base_family Base font family. Default "" (ggplot2 default). grid grid lines show. One "xy" (, default), "x" (vertical ), "y" (horizontal ), "none". legend_position Position legend passed ggplot2::theme(). Default "right".

title: Circadia Lab ggplot2 theme — theme_circadia
text: ggplot2::theme() object.

title: Circadia Lab ggplot2 theme — theme_circadia
code: library(ggplot2) ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg, colour = factor(cyl))) +   geom_point(size = 2) +   scale_colour_circadia() +   theme_circadia()
